_RETRACTED: 2026-07-01: produced by the BROKEN serial path (processes=1, __update_state_no_par). Near-floor accuracies are a serial-path isolation bug, NOT real recovery. Superseded by the parallel-path curve. See preprint §8.3 and memory/csa-serial-path-broken.md.

_original.note: Real measured CSA_OptMatcher recovery on downsampled known-answer benchmarks, run end-to-end via the validated batched cognate-matcher (CPU). Step budget 300-500 is UNDER-CONVERGED (linb reaches its published value only ~4000 steps), so every point is a LOWER BOUND and sits at/near the analytic chance floor. The converged accuracy-vs-size curve is compute-bound (CPU cost ~quadratic in lexicon size: linb sz100=4min, sz200=17min, sz900 would be hours; the rented H100 could not run the sweep). Reported as exercised-not-converged, not a result.
